title: Zeroizing<String> for access token with documented reqwest limitation
description: The access token is Zeroizing<String> so it zeroes on drop. The module doc honestly documents that reqwest copies the token into the Authorization header (non-zeroized) and that the response bytes buffer is not zeroized. This is the correct tradeoff: we own the zeroizing of what we can own; we document what we can't. A contributor might suggest removing Zeroizing because 'it doesn't help with reqwest' — that's wrong. Partial zeroization is better than none, and the documentation correctly scopes the guarantee.
